workspace.subtitle: The mobile flow works best when plan-day editing has its own dedicated space. Open a day to edit it in a wider builder instead of a cramped inline form.

create.title: Start a new plan day
create.subtitle: Open a dedicated route with enough space for the day name, exercise library, ordering, and summary.

edit.title: Edit in a full workspace
edit.subtitle: Each existing day opens in a wider editor inspired by the mobile flow.
edit.helper: Use the list on the left to open a plan day. The editor route gives more room for exercise picking and list management.

editor.subtitle: This editor only uses fields currently exposed by the plan day DTO: name, exercise, reps, and series.

plan.subtitle: This editor works inside the currently selected global training plan.

page.title: Manage your trainees
page.subtitle: Review relationship status, open member details, and keep the list filtered on the server for faster trainer workflows.

list.title: Your trainee relationships
list.subtitle: Search by name or email, narrow the list by status, sort on the server, and jump straight into the member workspace.

empty.title: No members to display
empty.subtitle: Try adjusting the filters or wait until the trainer-member relationships appear in the list.

error.title: Failed to load members
error.subtitle: The current filters could not be loaded. Try again in a moment.

actions.unlinkConfirm: Are you sure you want to unlink {name}?

feedback.loadFailed: Failed to load the members list.
feedback.unauthorized: Your trainer session expired or you no longer have access to this view.

page.title: Trainer relationship
page.subtitle: This screen relies only on the real invitation contract. It shows the public invitation status and lets you respond only when the API actually supports it.
guidance: If the invitation link contains a valid identifier and code, the screen loads the public invitation status generated by Orval.
Accept and reject actions are available only for the pending state supported by the accept/reject endpoints.
No invitation context does not automatically mean no relationship exists — it only means the current API contract does not give this view enough data to confirm it.

invitations.title: Invitations assigned to your account
invitations.subtitle: You have {count} pending trainer invitation. You can accept the relationship or reject the invitation without using the email link.
invitations.itemDescription: This invitation was sent to the email assigned to your account or directly to your athlete profile.
invitations.emptySubtitle: If a trainer has just sent an invitation, refresh this view. The list uses /api/trainee/invitations for the signed-in athlete.

summary.accountMeta: Identity comes from the active user session.
summary.contractMeta: This view only uses invitation / accept / reject endpoints.
summary.stateMeta: The state is derived only from the invitation API response.

actions.label: Available actions
actions.available: You can respond to the invitation
actions.pendingDescription: The API confirms a pending state, so you can accept or reject the relationship now.
actions.defaultDescription: Outside the pending state, this view stays informational and does not guess any extra capabilities.

identity.label: Athlete account
identity.description: This section uses only data available in the current session, without hardcoding trainer details.

scope.label: Source of truth
scope.description: The view does not use training-plan endpoints or any indirect relationship proxy.

contract.title: API contract
contract.primaryLine: Only invitation status and accept / reject endpoints are used.
contract.secondaryLine: If the backend does not expose a separate endpoint for the signed-in athlete's current relationship, this screen does not infer it from training plans or other modules.

items.statusMeta: The status field comes directly from PublicInvitationStatusDto.

items.codeMeta: The code is used only to fetch the public invitation status.
items.routeValue: Page opened from an invitation link

loading.title: Checking relationship status
loading.subtitle: This view is loading only invitation data exposed by the current API contract.

error.title: Failed to read invitation status
error.subtitle: Try again. If the problem keeps happening, the API did not return enough data to show the relationship state.

pending.title: You have a trainer invitation waiting
pending.subtitle: You can accept or reject this relationship now. This screen does not show trainer identity fields because the current DTO does not return them.

accepted.title: The invitation has been accepted
accepted.subtitle: The public invitation status returns an accepted state. The screen does not claim anything beyond what the API actually confirms.

rejected.title: This invitation was already rejected
rejected.subtitle: The invitation is no longer actionable and cannot be handled again from this view.

expired.title: This invitation is no longer active
expired.subtitle: The invitation status indicates that the link expired or was revoked, so there is no action left to take.

empty.title: No invitation data to display
empty.subtitle: This view has no active invitation context or the API did not return a status, so it cannot confirm a trainer-athlete relationship without guessing backend behavior.

feedback.loadFailed: Failed to load invitation or trainer relationship data.
feedback.invitationsEndpointMissing: The backend does not expose /api/trainee/invitations yet. Deploy the latest API branch so the athlete panel can load invitations without the email link.
feedback.acceptFailed: Failed to accept the trainer relationship.
feedback.rejectFailed: Failed to reject the trainer invitation.

header.snapshotHint: The page header uses the latest member snapshot already available in the trainer workspace.

overview.title: Member summary based on live trainer data
overview.subtitle: This section aggregates only data that already exists in the current API contract.

meta.trainingDates: Taken from the recorded training dates endpoint.
meta.noSnapshot: No cached snapshot available for this route yet.

error.title: Failed to build the overview
error.subtitle: At least one overview data source could not be loaded.

empty.title: No recorded training dates found.
empty.details: Select a date from the left to load training details.
empty.exercises: No exercises recorded for this training.

error.subtitle: Failed to load recorded training dates.
error.details: Failed to load the training details for the selected date.

plans.title: Trainer-managed plans
plans.subtitle: Create, rename, delete, assign, and unassign plans supported by the current trainer contract.

empty.title: No plans created for this member yet.

error.subtitle: Failed to load the trainer-managed plans.

feedback.unassignFailed: Failed to unassign the active plan.

reports.submissionsSubtitle: Request a report from this member and review all submitted answers.

empty.templates: No report templates are available yet. Create them in the global template library first.
empty.submissions: No report submissions found for this member.

feedback.templateRequired: Select a template before sending a request.

page.title: Report Templates
page.subtitle: Create, edit, preview, and delete reusable report templates for all your members from one trainer-level module.
page.summary: Templates are managed globally and reused when requesting reports from members.

feedback.templateFieldRequired: At least one valid template field is required.

measurements.subtitle: Track trend, history, and recent values using the existing measurement endpoints.

invitations.title: Invitations
invitations.subtitle: Invite new customers by email, track every status, and revoke pending invitations from one trainer view.

page.title: Manage collaboration invitations
page.subtitle: Send new invitations without leaving the trainer panel, then immediately review their status, history, and the next actions below.

form.title: Invite a new customer
form.subtitle: Provide the customer email together with the preferred language and time zone so the backend can create the invitation with the right defaults.
form.note: After a successful submit the form resets automatically and the invitation history on the right refreshes without a full page reload.

history.title: Your recent invitations
history.subtitle: Filter the list by status, search by email, and keep control over which invitations are still awaiting a response.

empty.title: No invitations to display
empty.subtitle: Try adjusting the filters or send the first invitation from the form on the left.

actions.revokeConfirm: Are you sure you want to revoke the invitation for {email}?

feedback.unauthorized: Your trainer session expired or you no longer have access to this view.

panel.title: Application management
panel.subtitle: Review all users, inspect their status, and assign roles directly from the new admin workspace.

side.title: Keep users, permissions, and workspace access under one sharp control surface.

users.label: Users & roles
users.title: Accounts and permissions
users.description: Add new users, update roles, and manage access from a single focused workspace.

versions.label: App versions
versions.title: Android and iOS releases
versions.description: Manage current mobile builds, minimum required versions, and update URLs for both Android and iOS.

register.title: Add a new user
register.subtitle: Create an athlete or trainer account directly from the administrator panel.
register.note: After creating the account, you can immediately assign extra roles from the user list below.

feedback.userAddedToList: User created and the list has been refreshed.

table.sectionSubtitle: Review account status, inspect assigned roles, and make access changes without leaving the dashboard.

feedback.unauthorized: Your administrator session expired or no longer has the required permissions. Please sign in again.

versions.title: Mobile update configuration
versions.subtitle: Create and refresh Android and iOS version settings using the Orval-generated app config endpoints.

fields.forceUpdateHint: Enable this when the app should block usage until the new version is installed.

placeholders.releaseNotes: Describe the main changes in this release

feedback.noConfig: No configuration has been saved for this platform yet.
feedback.loadFailed: Failed to load the app version configuration.
feedback.submitFailed: Failed to save the app version configuration.

notes.title: How to use it
notes.description: Both mobile platforms use the same API endpoint and are distinguished by the platform field in the payload. Load the current config first or overwrite it directly with a new Android or iOS release.
